You are running as a single automated phase of the ADW pipeline.
The orchestrator performs ALL git and GitHub work for this run: do NOT run git or gh, do NOT create/switch/commit/push branches, and do NOT open, merge, or comment on pull requests. If the task section below tells you to do any of that, skip those steps.
You have no GitHub access in this phase; all issue context you need is provided inline.

---

Classify work item #6 into a single change type for branch naming and commit prefixing.

Work item context (title, body, labels):

6 GitHub issue #6: fix: drift guard for ADW_* env naming in docs/prompts
Labels: issue_class:fix adw-live-batch

Part of the MVP live-run batch (`adw_sdlc/docs/LIVE-RUN-BATCH.md`). Intended `issue_class`: **fix**. Planned ADW run mode: native.

## Goal
Guard the recent env rename: canonical `ADW_*` names with deprecated `MX_AGENT_*` aliases. Prevent silent drift where docs/prompts reintroduce bare `MX_AGENT_*` as if canonical.

## Scope
- Add a lightweight check (script or vitest) asserting `src/` reads control-plane env only via the `env-vars.ts` alias helpers (no new bare `process.env['MX_AGENT_*']` reads outside `env-vars.ts`).
- Optionally scan committed docs/prompts for bare `MX_AGENT_*` not marked as a deprecated alias.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] The guard fails if a new bare `MX_AGENT_*` read is added to `src/` outside `env-vars.ts`.
- [ ] Existing canonical `ADW_*` usage passes.
- [ ] `npm run verify` stays green.

## Notes
Keep `MX_AGENT_*` working as aliases; this only prevents accidental regressions in naming.

## Instructions

- Choose exactly one `issue_class` from: `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `chore`, `ci`, `test`, `refactor`.
  - `feat` — new user-visible feature or capability.
  - `fix` — bug fix or correctness change.
  - `docs` — documentation-only change.
  - `chore` — maintenance, deps, tooling with no user-visible behavior change.
  - `ci` — CI/build/workflow change.
  - `test` — test-only change.
  - `refactor` — internal restructuring with no behavior change.
- Prefer the narrowest truthful classification.
- If labels and body disagree, classify by the actual requested change.
